The grizzly car accident in which 14-year-old Jacqueline Sanchez lost her life occurred on July 21st, 2017, just outside Los Banos, California. The ghoulish fact that her sister, Abdullia, live streamed the entire incident on Instagram would reveal the shocking reasons for the heartbreaking tragedy. When officers attempted to administer a breathalyzer test, Abdullia became violent and refused to comply. She was eventually restrained in the ambulance after she kicked an EMT in the face. However, Abdullia's suspected intoxication pales in comparison to what police found when they looked at her social media. They found some harrowing footage taken just that morning as Abdullia and Jacqueline made their way to Fresno. In the footage before the crash, Jacqueline can be heard telling Abdullia, you're going to kill me. Almost immediately after the crash, she started an Instagram live stream, which spared none of the gory details. If I go to jail for life, you already know why, Abdullia told her followers. My sister is dying. She then recorded her sister's mangled body for all to see, going so far as to zoom in on her head injury. Jacqueline was obviously deceased, but Abdullia proceeded to shake and roll her body in the grass, calling her name and begging her to wake up. Look, she said, I love my sister to death. I don't give a. This is the last thing I wanted to happen to us, but it just did. Even though the footage was later removed from social media, that didn't prevent it from being reposted and going viral. It was a central part of Abdullia's trial, along with hospital records that proved her blood alcohol content had been .106, well over the legal limit. Additional details about Abdullia's troubled past were also revealed, including the fact that she had reportedly been a victim of human trafficking and spent the past two years in a group home. In February 2018, Abdullia pleaded no contest to the felony charges of driving under the influence, gross vehicular manslaughter while intoxicated, and child endangerment. She was sentenced to six years in prison, but was released early on parole on September 21st, 2018. If one thought that the many charges against her were the end to this grim story, it was only the beginning of several others. Only 26 days later, she was in trouble with the police again after a regular traffic stop became a car chase. Police alleged that Abdullia was a felon in possession of a firearm, resisted arrest, and became violent towards officers. In 2022, Abdullia's social media accounts became active again, indicating that she was once again released from custody. On Instagram, she revealed to her followers that she had Jacqueline's name tattooed across her chest. Interestingly, it appears that everything on her account prior to the accident has been deleted. However, some of Abdullia's posts appear to have been saved and reuploaded. This selfie of Abdullia in her car was captioned, don't get in the car with me ever in your lifetime. More of Abdullia's posts revealed that while she's likely glad to be out of prison, she might not like some of the attention she's been getting. One of her posts read, people really be following you just to keep tabs on you, just watching 24/7. While another post read, all these weirdos on my page want to run mouth about my DUI, hop in my DMs and drop a pen. On a different note, she added the comment, got you babe, to this meme reading, when she fresh out of prison and makes dinner for you. She also added the comment, love to this picture of a T-shirt reading, free Abdullia Sanchez. Abdullia also posted this image of Jacqueline, trying on what might be the Quinceanera dress she never got to wear, and mentioned memorializing her sister for Dia de los Muertos. She followed up with this picture of her sister's grave, decorated for the occasion with the caption, rest easy until we meet again. She also talked about making special plans for her sister's birthday that year and shared this photo of Jacqueline beside a lake with the hashtag, long live Jackie. But there's a dark irony in the difference between Abdullia's posts about her sister and her day-to-day content. Abdullia also posted this video of herself with what appears to be alcohol in a vehicle. Her caption, it's all gone, raises concerns. Considering all of this, it might not be that surprising to hear that Abdullia was arrested yet again in 2023 after she was once again identified as a felon in possession of a firearm. She posted this image of her official jail ID with the caption, free me, shortly after her arrest. Tragically, Abdullia's arrests and run-ins with the law have not only affected her, but also the people closest to her, like her sister, Jacqueline.
